fact: November 6 is exactly 310 days into the year, which means there are only 55 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for squirrels to go absolutely nuts burying acorns - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're just getting home from school.
November 6 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season, when the mysterious scorpion rules the zodiac with its stinger ready to strike.
Maple trees are dropping their last colorful leaves right about now in northern climates, creating nature's most satisfying crunchy carpet for stomping.
Most spiders are actually dying off around this time after laying eggs that will hatch next spring - creepy but cool!
The first frost of winter typically hits many northern places within a week of November 6, turning morning grass into a glittery wonderland that melts by breakfast.
November 6 sits exactly 44 days after the autumn equinox, putting us deep into the season when nights grow longer than days.
Deer are usually in their mating season around this date, which makes them act super weird and bold - they might even wander into your backyard!
The moon phase around November 6 changes every year, but when it's a new moon, the sky gets so dark you can see way more stars than usual.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 6 is actually spring cleaning time for nature - flowers are blooming and baby animals are being born!
Morning fog becomes super common around this date because the air temperature and ground temperature are playing tug-of-war, creating those spooky misty mornings.
